title: Opening and managing bank accounts
description: Practice understanding of account management, from routing and account numbers, fees, to strategies for different scenarios.

assessment_items.question_content:
It’s important to know your routing and account numbers because you will need them to set up automatic payments.

**Look at the check below and match each number with the right label.**

[[☃ label-image 1]]

options.imageAlt: A check from ABC National Bank. On the bottom is a series of numbers. The first set of numbers is 9 digits, followed by a space and a set of 12 digits, followed by a space and 4 digits. In the top right corner is a 4 digit number 0001.

markers.label: the second set of numbers, 12 digits long
the first set of series of numbers, 9 digits long
4 digits on the top-right corner of a check

assessment_items.question_content:
You recently had a few purchases that caused your account balance to turn negative. As a result, your bank charged you a fee.

**What fee were you most likely charged?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: monthly maintenance fee
overdraft fee
check cashing fee
ATM fee

assessment_items.question_content:
You recently made a few purchases that brought your account balance to zero. As a result, your bank charged you a fee.

**What fee were you most likely charged?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: monthly maintenance fee
overdraft fee
check cashing fee
ATM fee

assessment_items.question_content:
You recently checked your bank statement and noticed a monthly maintenance fee. Your account balance is positive.


**What could be the reason you are being charged a maintenance fee?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: You forgot to set up direct deposit.
You used another bank's ATM.
You made too many withdrawals in a month.
Your account is overdrawn.

assessment_items.question_content:
You’ve noticed on your bank statement there is a $\$15$ monthly maintenance fee. 

**What would be the best solution to avoid paying this fee?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: withdraw your money and close the account
call your bank and negotiate your fees
meet minimum balance, set up direct deposit, or meet other bank requirements
transfer money frequently between accounts

assessment_items.question_content:
You forgot to pay utilities and were charged a late fee. 

**What would be the best solution to avoid paying such fees?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: set money aside every month for your utility bill
set up automatic payments
make more money
set a reminder in your calendar to pay the bill before it is due

assessment_items.question_content:
Your employer pays you with a check. The bank charges you a fee when you cash it.

**What would be the best solution to avoid paying this fee?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: set up direct deposit
use a check cashing service
cash your check at a different  bank
open a new bank account

assessment_items.question_content:
You're out shopping and decide to pick up a few things you need. You approach the cash register, not sure if your checking account has enough to cover the total. If your account does come up short, your bank will charge you an overdraft fee.

**What can you do in the future to prevent an overdraft fee?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Pay with a different card every time you shop.
Check your account balance regularly and before you get to the cash register.
Only shop on weekends.
Ask the cashier to check your account balance.

assessment_items.question_content:
You’ve had to pay overdraft fees before and want to avoid them the next time you check your bank statement. 

**How can you prevent these fees?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Withdraw all of your money and pay in cash.
Always carry your bank’s fee schedule with you.
Set up an alert to notify you when your balance is low.
Shop online instead of in-store to avoid fees.

assessment_items.question_content:
It's been a busy month with lots of unexpected expenses, and you fear your checking account might run low. 

**What’s the best action to avoid the worry about overdraft fees?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Pay for everything with a check since checks can't overdraft.
Link a savings account or credit card to your checking account for overdraft protection.
Try to remember to check your account once a month.
Cut up all your cards so you can't overspend.
